reviews.review_title: Paid for Pro Edition, locked again
reviews.review_description: On the first day, I paid $9.99 for the Pro Edition, and all the game modes were unlocked. But on the second and third day, two game modes were locked again for no reason. This is very frustrating and feels unfair because I already paid for the full version. I’m leaving a one-star review for now. If you fix this issue, I’ll be willing to change my review.

developer_response.body:
This issue was caused by a bug in an older version of the game (0.1.3).

It has since been fully fixed, and purchases now remain permanently unlocked as expected.

reviews.review_title: Amazing (i don't use that very often)
reviews.review_description: love it. It has taught me to box and i have won some tournaments because of this app P S I also want the free gloves.
this was a real workout. definitely a good way to get in shape.  i highly recommend.

I've tried pretty much all boxing type VR fitness games so far, and have a FitXR sub.

This game has a more realistic "boxing type movement flow" than all the others. 
It targets the core and abs way better than other games👍

Content is limited so far, but worth the supporter fee, imho.

Only request would be an invincible/training mode. 
If I miss, just play a sound and let me continue.

Looking forward to content/feature updates.

Regards

Btw, love the MR only mode. Gives you more time to focus on new workouts.
I'd recommend a split of default whole body workouts, and then some lower body workouts with focus on short ducks/squats and lunges.

locking setting behind a pro version? and half of the game modes behind a pro version? Crazy work having to restart game after getting to pay wall too is another thing

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback!

There was actually a bug that prevented players from exiting the paywall screen — thanks for pointing it out, it’s already fixed.

The core game is free to play, and Pro is meant to unlock extra modes, not block your experience.

reviews.review_description: Barely any work put into

Was looking for a fitness game that fits my requirements. 
1: able to be played from a chair if required ( I have low mobility days but still want to do movement based games)
2: Inexpensive ( I’m a senior on a fixed income) and this was free.
3: DOESNT SPAWN ME UP IN THE AIR/ON A ROOFTOP! ( yeah, that’s an issue with far too many game designers. I mean what’s the purpose? Are game devs all sadists or unfamiliar with acrophobia? If you have to include dizzying platforms at least take a lesson from Beat saber and let players select PRIOR to the start of the game!)

4: guess which is my major issue with other games…and one of the major pluses of this one?

Now as to the game, it’s clean. Simple mechanics, and the graphics don’t get lost in the MR background. Bright yellow targets and red bombs to dodge while hitting is as clear cut as you could ask for. Even though you’re wearing boxing gloves I liken this more to a dodgeball game. Hit the ones coming towards you , dodge the bombs while still striking .  Simple and I still was sweating afterwards.

I like it.
Very usefull if u want to exercise
Pretty good exercise

I've been playing vr for years and always wished there was a free rhythm boxing game that was free or cheep, I haven't looked in a long time and then find this gem! All game modes are fun especially rhythm. But pads with my own music is amazing! Thanks for this. Definitely buy the supporter gloves its worth every penny!

I really like the game, I have checked many boxing games. For me, this comes first. I was looking for a game with driving beat music and an active mode so that it wouldn't be boring. Not everyone has similar modes. This game combines cool music and interesting modes. What I'm happy with, the subscription is definitely worth it. Different ages will like the game, I'm 25 years old, my brother is 10, and even my mother is 45. It's a great way to unwind after a stressful day. Well, I definitely like the songs, they are creative and interesting.
Good
this game is amazing for losing weight

reviews.review_title: Lots of fun when it works
reviews.review_description:
I love this game when I can get the thing to run right, which is about 25% of the time.
I have a new Meta 3, so its not the hardware. 

The game itself is screwy. For starters, the gloves dont even remotely line up, no matter what. U can look at ur hands and see that the gloves are all of 3 inches off center. Its infuriating, cuz itll tell me i missed things that I definitely hit, and its all throughout, not just when it gets going fast. Drives me nuts. 
Plus with every round everything slides up or down steadily, so after 3 or 4 rounds im either always reaching for the floor or the ceiling, and it only gets fixed by closing and reopening the app. 
Again, absolutely infuriating. 

I would love to pay a lil extra for the xtra game modes, cuz they look fun, but they got no shot until they make the base game work.

This game is amazing, and not one review should be less than 5, especially as it's free. However, I only have one discomfort issue, which is primarily my fault for not updating my headset. It's the fact that Quest 2 has no colors in pass-through, and I'm forced to play black & white alongside weak resolution whilst playing. So, a suggestion is to add built-in environment(s) with color as a toggle.

I'm an MMA fighter, and this is by far the most fun boxing experience I've ever had in VR (and I love TOTF 1)

The Great:

While I find the main game mode enjoyable, the pad work is truly engaging and immersive. You end up throwing Jabs, crosses, hooks, uppercuts, slips, rolls! And with the haptic feedback, it really feels like you're landing shots on a pad!

Potential improvements/additions:

I feel the pad work section could benefit from a left hand/right hand mode (orthodox/southpaw) because in my stance, I'm prompted to throw my rear hand twice in a row often, which is atypical in striking and feels a bit unnatural white throwing off my rhythm.

Additionally, I feel if there was a glove calibration feature, it could improve accuracy, especially on hooks.

Conclusion:

That being said, this game is awesome. Save your money on TOTF 2 garbage, and pick up this free game instead!
Its supa fun and stuff but i feel like before u just buy the globes u shouldnget like a preview to get to play it like once or twice or for like 15 minutes but other than that amazing

Best game , free or otherwise I've installed since Hyper Dash. Get it ! More than worth the space on your headset 
Of it's kind, definitely one of the better ones and free. Set at a good standard, not so easy it's boring and challenging enough to give that big yellow ball a dig to start again without thinking.
I will definitely support financialy when ;

Haven't played around with the music yet. But working out to your own music/playlists would be ace
More game options. The request for suggestions is something I'll be back with.
basically duolingo for boxing pretty good game repetitive but fun.

What I'm missing is the ability to set up a game with my own settings.
*most importantly: the possibility to choose if you want to play with lives or endless lives. Even if I make a mistake I want to keep working out without interruption, a short error sound or visual signal would be enough
*choose your own playing time (x minutes, endless)
*choose the speed level like constant speed, with increasing speed (also have the ability to choose beginning/maximum speed level), ...

I like the leaderboard. I like the punch pads. It made the game a little better, but there are a few things that can make it better. The punch pads are slow. You got to speed it up or make the spacing between each move shooter.

I say if it's a short combo speed it up. Make it like a short burst where your combos got to be fast and your punches got to be sharp. 

If it's a long combo keep it the same speed, but make the spacing between each move shorter. The way the game is now during long combos. I feel like I'm waiting 2 seconds to long for the next move. 

The update on the other mode. You got to bring up the sensitivity of the ball. The old version was way better. The way it is now if you got to force a punch to pop a ball. It can lead to that person getting injured.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your suggestions! Agree the sensitivity needs to be higher, will work on that.
Join us on Discord:
https://boxing-minigames.com/discord

Looking forward to all the upgrades & new songs! 🥊💥🥊💢

I have over a decade of boxing experience and i've tried just about every VR boxing game... Duck or Die has the best pad holding accuracy by far. Maybe once or twice a round it gives you a combo where the slip doesn't match the counter, but thats my only complaint.

reviews.review_description:
Puch pads are great! Exactly what I was looking for: a shadow boxing training game with realistic combinations and dodges, instead of another stupid fitness punching game that there are so many on the market. It's great that you can slip the flying glove and not have to block it. Boxing is not about mindless spamming punches, but it's also a lot about dodging. Combinations like - slip - punch - weave - slip and load for a puch - punch - punch - duck.

The original duck-or-die game is still great for reflexes.

I'll give you more feedback and tips on Discord.

reviews.review_title: a lot of work needs polishing

developer_response.body:
PRO just launched and we’re actively polishing it!

If there’s something specific that didn’t meet your expectations, I’d love to hear it and make it right. You can reach me anytime on Discord.

The game is free to play because I want as many people as possible to enjoy it.

reviews.review_title: One of the best workouts in VR
reviews.review_description:
I have played a few of the other VR boxing games. Thrill of the fight 1 is the best full on boxing simulation, but workout wise, this is amazing.

The reflex ball mode is pretty fun, but I'm really impressed by the pads mode. I've been boxing for about 10 years now. I'm impressed by how well real combos fit into the pad mode. The pads line up appropriately when ducking under the bar. Sometimes I feel like I'm hitting legitimate combos and other times it feels totally random. Either way, it's super fun.

The pacing in the pad mode is perfect. It starts slow and comfortable, but picks up as you advance and gets to a point where it becomes a great workout. When you're trying it over and over again, that slow start is a nice break. 

This is a fantastic idea. The music mode sounds really cool but I haven't pulled the trigger on paying yet. I will update my review if I do.
fun mini-game
Its pretty fun but its too easy hopefully updates will bring more game modes.
What a work out. It's really cool simple but hard beat your own score and get a work out
